paragraphs.body: The heart of the city: Point State Park vs QuanCheng Square  Exposition and Conflict: Before visiting Point State Park in Pittsburgh, I had a very limited imagination of it. I expected to see a large and ordinary urban green space surrounded by various urban buildings. But when I actually visited Point State Park, my senses were greatly shocked. Standing in the center of the park, I found myself facing three large rivers and two tall bridges. It was not a small green space in the city as I expected. First, what is sitting by the river is a huge fountain, and the enormous size of the park shocked me as well. If I wasn’t there once I could not believe how come there’s a heavenly place located downtown, its grandeur vegetation and scale made me feel immersed in the forest. The existence of point state park for Pittsburgh just like the Central Park for New York, all natural elements are well integrated with the urban landscape. This reminded me that there was a similar square in the downtown of my hometown in Jinan. Jinan, located in China, is also a city with a long history of steel industry. (AI image of what I actually see the Point State Park)
(AI image of the feeling of Quancheng Square)

After comparing Point State Park and Quancheng Square in Jinan, I found the similarities and differences between them. Both are central landmarks of the city, and both are built with water as the core element. Point State Park is located on the edge of the river and has a fountain, while Quancheng Square in my hometown uses spring water as the concept of construction and also has a huge fountain. After living in Pittsburgh for three years, I also think that these two places are crucial to the history of their respective cities and the identity of their residents. They both carry the epitome of their respective urban construction, development and people's lives. The building of the Steelworkers Union still stands next to Point State Park. As a symbol of the times, it always reminds people of the city's past glory. For Quancheng Square, the Jinan Incident Massacre Memorial(五卅惨案纪念碑)on the west side of the square records the bloody incident of Japanese imperialism slaughtering Chinese soldiers, civilians and diplomatic officials here, and Its existence will always warn future generations of the suffering of war.

The main difference between the two is in building scale. After comparing the data, I found that Point State Park is much larger than Quancheng Square and has more vegetation. It's hard to imagine such a huge place to enjoy natural scenery in such a modern city. People in Pittsburgh prefer to engage in various outdoor activities in the park, such as running. Due to the population base, the already enormous park appears quite empty from Monday to Friday. In Jinan, people hold various cultural performances in the square, which is also a major characteristic of Chinese cities. People fly kites in the square, and the elderly dance in groups(广场舞). And I can merely find greens around the square beside the BaoTu Spring Garden nearby. As I see, the two landmarks have been given different functions by their respective populations.
( AI image of comparison of Quancheng Square and Point State Park)

The comparison indeed changed my understanding of the purpose for urban planning. I originally thought that the public activity space in the city center was mainly to beautify the overall environment of the city and add some natural colors to a modern city. But through comparison, I found that these two spots play an important role in shaping urban identity, preserving historical memory and promoting mutual communication between people. In addition, I re-realized that two completely different urban planning schemes can achieve similar social functions. They each inherit the glorious identity and history of the city in the past and pass on such emotions to the people living in the city today.
